calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Set For Trial On Aug-10-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MOUNT ST. JOSEPH-ST. ELIZABETH's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendant Mount St. Joseph-St. Elizabeth dba The Epiphany Center's motion to strike is granted. The court strikes the claim for punitive damages pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 425.14. Plaintiff must obtain a court order before seeking that relief from defendant.

Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 425.14 provides that a plaintiff must obtain a court order before seeking punitive damages against a "religious corporation." The court takes judicial notice of defendant's Articles of Incorporation and Bylaws. Those documents, which plaintiff agrees the court can take judicial notice of, indisputably show that defendant is a religious corporation subject to the protections of section 425.14. Section 1 of the Bylaws states that the philosophy of the corporation is "in accordance with the official teachings of the Roman Catholic Church." Section 3 of the Bylaws states that "[t]his corporation is formed for the purpose of carrying out the mission of the DOC which is to imitate the love of Jesus Christ?"

The court rejects plaintiff's argument that the statute does not apply because defendant is a "charitable nonprofit corporation" or public benefit corporation under Corporations Code Sec. 5110 et seq. and not a "religious corporation" under Corporations Code Sec. 9110 et seq.

Defendant was incorporated in 1976 and at that time it was a religious corporation or organization. The Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law (Cal. Corp. Code, Sec. 5110 et seq.) and Nonprofit Religious Corporation Law (Cal. Corp. Code, Sec. 9110 et seq.) went into effect in 1980.

Cal. Corp. Code, Sec. 5003(b) states that: The existence of corporations formed or existing on the date of enactment or reenactment of this part, Part 2, Part 3, Part 4 or Part 5 shall not be affected by the enactment or reenactment of such parts or by any change in the requirements for the formation of corporations or by the amendment or repeal of the laws under which they were formed or created.

Plaintiff basically argues that defendant needed to reclassify itself in response to the new laws, but that is inconsistent with section 5003(b). (See also Cal. Corp. Code 9912(a)(2) [any corporation organized primarily for religious purposes under prior law shall be subject to the new religious corporation law].)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
